title: sisymbrium viscosum
intro: Sisymbrium viscosum, also known as sticky marigold or viscid marigold, is a plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. The health benefits of Sisymbrium viscosum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Sisymbrium viscosum can be made into infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, taken orally as capsules or tablets, or applied topically to the skin as poultices or creams.
